methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $690M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bethlehem Steel Corp is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $690M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), with notable peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, though no direct narrow-stage comparables exist at sufficient scale.

The Stage-Progression method applies here, using the company's $690M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $828M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) returned insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3 matches required for statistical reliability), making a sector-wide median unreliable.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $704M to $952M, with a median point of $828M. This narrow methodology base represents the primary analytical constraint. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable companies at identical stage and valuation tiers, limiting cross-validation, and dependence on the stage-progression multiplier's appropriateness to this specific company's growth trajectory and market dynamics. Additional data on revenue scale, growth rates, or capital efficiency relative to peers would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
